RESOURCE CENTER

Manufacturing Knowledge, Cases and Materials

Reference position for the entire process of R&D and production of precision parts. Here you can consult the detailed description of the processing process, material performance parameters, real parts cases, and download standardized engineering materials. From DFM review of parts design, process selection, material comparison, to quotation communication, shipment acceptance, one-stop access to industrial manufacturing reference information to efficiently promote the implementation of parts projects.

Detailed Design Specification

Precision parts DFM manufacturability criteria, collate structural optimization, tolerance labeling, drawings specification points, reduce the difficulty of processing and additional costs caused by design.

Download center

MODEL COMMUNITY

3D Model Community

Browse, download, and share 3D models of industrial parts for CNC machining, 3D printing, sheet metal, injection molding, and foundry processes. The platform is compatible with mainstream 3D formats such as step, IGES, STL, and OBJ, and can be used by R&D engineers to refer to mature part structures and evaluate design manufacturability. You can also upload your own 3D models and exchange structural design ideas with industry practitioners.

PROCESS GUIDE

Process Guide

Gather the mainstream precision manufacturing process knowledge base to provide model selection reference for R&D and procurement engineers. You can quickly understand the shape, material range, accuracy upper limit and surface treatment capacity of each processing method, and complete the process comparison in the early stage of the project to avoid problems such as design unable to be processed, excessive cost, and uncontrolled delivery cycle.

Process Catalog

01CNC machining 023D Printing 03Sheet metal processing 04Injection moulding 05casting technique 06Surface Finishing
PROCESS 01

CNC machining

CNC CNC machining relies on CNC program-driven tools to complete material removal processing. It can achieve complex cavity, special-shaped curved surface, and high-precision hole system structure molding. It not only supports single-piece sample trial production, medium and small batch trial production, but also can undertake stable mass production. It is widely used in metal and high-performance engineering plastic precision parts manufacturing.

Applicable materials:

Aluminum alloy, stainless steel, carbon steel, copper alloy, titanium alloy; pom, peek, nylon, PC, acrylic and other engineering plastics. The material comparison and selection can be completed according to the load of the parts, the use environment, and the lightweight requirements.

Precision Range

Conventional mass production machining accuracy ± 0.05 mm; precision machining grade up to ± 0.01 mm; ultra-high precision customized machining up to ± 0.005 mm. The wall thickness, structural shape, and clamping scheme of the part will affect the final achievable accuracy.

Surface roughness

Standard milling surface Ra 0.8–3.2μm; finishing can reach Ra 0.4μm; with polishing process can achieve Ra 0.2μm mirror effect. The roughness directly affects the assembly sealing and friction characteristics of the parts, as well as the subsequent surface treatment effects such as anodizing and electroplating.

Typical Delivery Time

3–7 days for conventional structural parts; 7–14 days for multi-process complex special-shaped parts; 14–30 days for mold cavity molds and ultra-high-precision customized parts. Coordinated delivery plans can be arranged according to orders.

Process

  1. Drawing ReviewCard the drawing size reference, tolerance requirements, and assembly conditions, and identify structural processing difficulties and design risks in advance.
  2. Process planningPlan a complete machining process, select suitable tools and tooling solutions, and balance the processing cost, processing efficiency, and finished product quality.
  3. Programming SimulationCam programming and path simulation to avoid the risk of machining overcutting and knife striking, and to ensure machining safety.
  4. First trial cutComplete the full-scale inspection of the first prototype, confirm that the dimensional accuracy and appearance quality are up to standard, and solidify and stabilize the processing technology.
  5. Mass productionContinuous sampling and testing during the mass production stage, relying on standardized process control to ensure the consistency of the dimensions of high-volume parts.
See the full CNC guide →
PROCESS 02

3D Printing

Additive manufacturing technology for rapid prototyping and small batch customization through layer-by-layer stacking of materials to form complex geometries.

Process type

SLA (light curing), SLS (selective laser sintering), FDM (fusion deposition), SLM (metal laser melting)

Precision Range

SLA ±0.1mm，SLS ±0.2mm，FDM ±0.3mm，SLM ±0.05mm

Layer Thickness Selection

0.025mm (micro-nano grade), 0.05-0.1mm (precision grade), 0.2-0.3mm (standard grade)

Typical Delivery Time

1-3 days (Rapid Prototyping), 3-7 days (Functional Parts), 7-14 days (Metal Parts)

Printing Process

  1. Model PreparationSTL file repair, wall thickness check and support addition
  2. SlicingLayer thickness, fill rate and print direction optimization
  3. Printer MonitorReal-time temperature, interlayer adhesion and deformation control
  4. Post ProcessingRemoval of support, surface polishing and heat treatment
  5. Quality TestsDimensional scanning, density testing and mechanical properties verification
View the full 3D printing guide →
PROCESS 03

Sheet metal processing

Laser cutting, CNC bending and stamping for efficient production of sheet metal parts.

Applicable materials:

Cold rolled steel plate, stainless steel plate, aluminum plate, copper plate, thickness 0.5-6mm

Precision Range

Cutting ± 0.1mm, Bending ± 0.2mm, Cavity ± 0.05 mm

Surface Finishing

Spraying, plating, anodizing, drawing, passivation

Typical Delivery Time

2-5 days (simple parts), 5-10 days (complex welds)

Process

  1. Drawing ReviewConfirm unfold size, bending order and tolerance requirements
  2. Laser cuttingHigh-precision cutting and cutting to ensure dimensional accuracy
  3. CNC bendingSequential bending and forming to control springback
  4. Welding AssemblySpot welding or arc welding if necessary
  5. Surface FinishingGrinding, spraying or plating
See the full sheet metal guide →
PROCESS 04

Injection moulding

Thermoplastic melt high pressure injection mold cavity, suitable for mass production of plastic parts.

Applicable materials:

ABS, PP, PC, PA, pom, peek, TPE, etc.

Precision Range

General grade ± 0.1mm, precision grade ± 0.05 mm, ultra-precision grade ± 0.02 mm

Mold life

100,000 times (aluminum mold), 300,000-500,000 times (steel mold), 1 million times (hard mold)

Typical Delivery Time

7-14 days (hand plate), 20-35 days (soft die), 35-60 days (hard die)

Molding process

  1. Mold designDetermine parting surface, gate position and cooling system
  2. Mold makingCNC machining, EDM and polishing
  3. Mold Test AdjustmentOptimize process parameters, verify dimensions and appearance
  4. Mass productionStabilize processes, control cycles and quality
View full injection molding guide →
PROCESS 05

casting technique

Pour the molten metal into the mold to obtain a blank, suitable for complex shapes and large metal pieces.

Process type

Sand casting, investment casting, die casting, centrifugal casting, continuous casting

Applicable materials:

Cast iron, cast steel, aluminum alloy, copper alloy, zinc alloy, magnesium alloy

Precision Range

CT7-CT9 (sand casting), CT4-CT6 (investment mold), CT3-CT5 (die casting)

Typical Delivery Time

15-30 days (sand casting), 20-45 days (investment mold), 10-20 days (die casting)

Casting process

  1. mouldingMaking of wood, metal or wax molds
  2. Sand/ShellStyling, Coremaking & Drying
  3. Smelting PouringMetal melting, refining and casting
  4. Cleaning inspectionRemoval of casting risers, sandblasting and non-destructive testing
View full casting guide →
PROCESS 06

Surface Finishing

A post-processing process that improves the appearance, corrosion resistance and functional properties of parts.

Applicable materials:

Aluminum alloy, stainless steel, carbon steel, copper alloy, titanium alloy, engineering plastics

Process type

Anodizing, plating, spraying, powder spraying, heat treatment, laser marking, polishing, passivation

Film Thickness Range

Anodic oxidation 5-25μm, electroplating 3-50μm, spraying 40-120μm

Typical Delivery Time

2-5 days (standard parts), 5-10 days (complex parts), 7-14 days (special processes)

Process flow

  1. Surface pretreatmentDegreasing, pickling, grinding to remove oxide and impurities
  2. Process implementationSurface treatment by selected process
  3. Quality TestsFilm thickness, hardness, adhesion and corrosion resistance tests
View full surface treatment guide →
MATERIAL HANDBOOK

Precision Machining Materials Manual

Bring together the physical parameters of commonly used metals and engineering plastics in machining, combined with CNC, sheet metal, 3D printing, The injection molding process features are compared with the material selection scheme to help you accurately match the working conditions of the parts and reduce the cost of trial and error.

Complete material parameters, processing recommendations, cost comparison Access to Material Brochure→
01
Metal material selection Mainstream machined metal grades such as aluminum alloy, stainless steel, titanium alloy, copper, and carbon steel are included. Covers mechanical properties, temperature-resistant and corrosion-resistant properties, cutting difficulty, adaptation to automation, medical, Reference for selection of aerospace components.
02
Engineering Plastics Selection Summarize the parameters of commonly used engineering plastics such as peek, pom, nylon, PC, acrylic, distinguish between wear-resistant, Insulation, high temperature resistance, lightweight application scenarios, providing CNC cutting and molding processing points to note.
03
Material Process Matching Guide Combined with different processing methods to analyze the material suitability, comparing the same material in CNC processing, sheet metal, Molding limitations, surface treatment capacity and cost differences under 3D printing process.
04
Material Selection Case Reference Selected real-life material selection cases for various parts and components, comparing the use environment and load demand for re-disc materials The choice of ideas provides a mature material selection reference for similar new projects.

If you have questions about material selection, program evaluation, you canContact a process engineerGet a free consultation.

DESIGN GUIDELINES

Design Specifications for Precision Components

From engineering drawings, dimensional tolerances, part structures to the design boundaries of different manufacturing processes, Identify key design issues affecting machining costs, accuracy, quality and lead times in advance.

Enter Design Specification→
01

Engineering drawings and dimensioning

Specify dimensional benchmarks, view expressions, cavities, threads, surface roughness and technical requirements, Reduce drawings ambiguity and omission of machining information.

View Drawing Specifications → 02

Tolerance & Assembly Design

Set the dimensional tolerance, shape and position tolerance and matching relationship reasonably according to the function of the part, Avoid excessive tolerances that increase processing difficulty and cost.

View tolerance specifications → 03

Manufacturing process structure specification

Comb through wall thicknesses, rounded corners, deep cavities, Inverted buckle, bending and support structure design boundaries.

View process specifications → 04

DFM Inspection & Data Delivery

Identify tool accessibility, clamping deformation, thin wall risk, Processing benchmark and post-processing margin issues.

View DFM Guide →

HELP & FAQ

Help FAQs

Summarize parts quotation, drawing preparation, material selection, process evaluation, quality acceptance and order delivery Frequently asked questions to help R&D and procurement staff quickly confirm pre-project requirements and reduce duplicate communication.

See all FAQs→
01 What information do I need to prepare before submitting a quote?

It is recommended to provide 3D models or 2D engineering drawings, material requirements, quantity, surface treatment methods, Key dimensional tolerances and parts usage scenarios.The more complete the information, the more accurate the quotation and process evaluation results.

02 What if I am unsure of the material and process?

Part loads, operating temperatures, corrosive environments, weight limits and budget requirements are available, The process engineer will assist in completing the scheme comparison by combining CNC, sheet metal, 3D printing, injection molding and other methods.

03 Does it support sample prototyping and small batch production?

Support from single sample, rapid prototyping to small and medium batch trial production and stable mass production. The machining protocol, testing requirements and delivery rhythm can be adjusted according to the project validation stage.

04 What quality and acceptance information is available?

The first inspection report, dimensional inspection records, material certificates, Surface treatment reports and other agreed quality documents, subject to quotation and order requirements.

Looking for part design and machining references?

Review process parameters, material selection standards and DFM design specifications; upload drawing models to obtain accurate machining quotations at the same time.
